name: Better Stack API
what_it_does: Better Stack — unified observability platform combining structured log management (Logtail), uptime monitoring, and incident management with a REST API for querying logs and managing monitors.
use_cases: Agents querying structured logs in real time via Better Stack's SQL-based log query API for debugging and analytics
Uptime monitoring automation — agents creating and managing monitors for agent services and APIs
Incident response — agents receiving webhook alerts and triggering automated remediation on uptime failures
Log-based alerting — agents setting up alert policies that trigger when log patterns match anomalies
Structured log ingestion — agents shipping application logs with custom metadata for searchable audit trails
not_for: Full APM with distributed tracing — use Datadog or New Relic for trace-level performance monitoring
Very high volume log ingestion (>100GB/day) — enterprise logging solutions scale better at extreme volume
Real-time streaming log analytics — Better Stack is query-based, not streaming
best_when: Your agent stack needs integrated logging and uptime monitoring with a developer-friendly API, SQL log queries, and incident alerting without the complexity of Datadog.
avoid_when: You need distributed tracing, full APM, or extremely high log volume — use Datadog, Grafana, or Elastic.

auth.notes: Bearer token from Better Stack dashboard. Separate tokens for Uptime API and Logtail API. Tokens are account-wide — no scope granularity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Uptime: 10 monitors free; Logtail: 1GB/month free logs, 3-day retention
pricing.paid_tiers: Uptime Starter: $24/month; Logtail: $0.05/GB ingested; bundles available
pricing.notes: Generous free tiers for both products. Log volume-based pricing for Logtail. Uptime monitoring seat/monitor-based pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Uptime API: 30 req/min; Logtail query API: 10 req/min; log ingestion: high throughput

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear REST API documentation. Error responses include specific messages. SQL log query errors return syntax-level detail. Rate limit errors include Retry-After headers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Monitor creation is idempotent with same URL. Log ingestion is not idempotent — duplicate logs are stored as separate entries. Incident creation is automatic from monitor state.
known_agent_gotchas: Uptime API and Logtail API use different base URLs and separate auth tokens — easy to confuse
Logtail query rate limit (10 req/min) is low for agents running frequent log analytics
Log retention varies by plan — agents querying historical data may get empty results on free tier (3-day retention)
Monitor check intervals have a minimum (30s) — agents cannot create sub-30s uptime monitoring
Webhook payloads for incidents and monitors differ — agents must handle both formats for unified incident processing

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. GDPR compliant. EU data residency option. TLS enforced. Bearer tokens with no scope granularity is a limitation for least-privilege access.
